dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 5929 RH: Critical Minerals Supply Chain Resiliency Act of 2026
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mr. Barr introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Natural Resources
Additional sponsors: Mr. Swalwell and Mr. Van Drew
Reported with an amendment, committed to the Committee of the Whole House on the State of the Union, and ordered to be printed
action.action-instruction: Strike out all after the enacting clause and insert the part printed in italic
For text of introduced bill, see copy of bill as introduced on November 7, 2025

form.official-title: To adjust the treatment of certain actions under Presidential Determination 2022–11 for Federal permitting improvement, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Critical Minerals Supply Chain Resiliency Act of 2026.

section.header: Treatment of actions under presidential determination 2022–11 for Federal permitting improvement purposes

subsection.text: Except as provided by subsection (c), an action described in subsection (b) shall be—

paragraph.text: treated as a covered project, as defined in section 41001(6) of the Fixing America’s Surface Transportation Act (42 U.S.C. 4370m(6)), without regard to the requirements of that section; and
included in the Permitting Dashboard maintained pursuant to section 41003(b) of that Act (42 U.S.C. 4370m–2(b)).

subsection.text: An action described in this subsection is an action taken by the Secretary of Defense pursuant to Presidential Determination 2022–11 (87 Fed. Reg. 19775; relating to certain actions under section 303 of the Defense Production Act of 1950) to create, maintain, protect, expand, or restore sustainable and responsible domestic production capabilities for strategic and critical materials through—

paragraph.text: supporting feasibility studies for mature mining, beneficiation, and value-added processing projects;
by-product and co-product production at existing mining, mine waste reclamation, and other industrial facilities;
modernization of mining, beneficiation, and value-added processing to increase productivity, environmental sustainability, and workforce safety; or
any other activity authorized under section 303(a)(1) of the Defense Production Act of 1950 (50 U.S.C. 4533(a)(1)).

subsection.text: An action described in subsection (b) may not be treated as a covered project or be included in the Permitting Dashboard under subsection (a) if the project sponsor (as defined in section 41001(18) of the Fixing America’s Surface Transportation Act (42 U.S.C. 4370m(18))) requests that the action not be treated as a covered project.

endorsement.action-desc: Reported with an amendment, committed to the Committee of the Whole House on the State of the Union, and ordered to be printed
